issues.title: How to Talk to Your Child About Failure
issues.body:
## Reframing Failure

One of the most powerful gifts you can give your child is a healthy relationship with failure. Teaching children that failure is a normal part of growth is deeply important.

### Why We Fear Failure

Most adults are uncomfortable with failure because they were never taught to process it well. We pass this discomfort to our children without realising it.

### What to Say Instead

Next time your child fails, resist the urge to reassure them immediately. Sit with them in the disappointment first. Then ask: _What do you think you could do differently next time?_

### This Month's Challenge

Share a story of your own failure with your child this week — something real. Then tell them what you learned from it.

issues.title: Screen Time: Finding the Balance That Works for Your Family
issues.body:
## Screens and Children

Screen time is one of the most contested parenting topics of our generation. The research is more nuanced than the headlines suggest.

### What the Research Actually Says

The quality of screen time matters far more than the quantity. A child watching an educational programme with a parent who discusses it with them is having a very different experience from a child scrolling alone.

![](/images/slide/adults-kids.jpg "Kids")

### Practical Boundaries That Stick

Rules work best when children understand the why behind them. Instead of just saying no screens after 8pm, explain that screens before bed affect their sleep and focus.

### One Thing to Try This Month

Create one daily screen-free window as a family — even just 30 minutes at dinner. Start small and build from there.
